intro: Hepetis trianae, also known as Lantana, is a flowering plant species native to the Americas, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. The leaves and flowers of Lantana have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its purported health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Lantana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lantana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
